title: Streamline implementation of SystemC mode
body:
I find myself coming into conflict with the SystemC mode often while trying to make changes to the model structure. I would like to know if you would accept a change which generates the SystemC module as an add-on wrapper around the C module, instead of the current way of emitting something different. In other words: `verilator --sc` would do the same as `verilator --cc` + generate an additional SC module which wraps an instance of the `--cc` module.

In practice this might require copying the IO signals and structuring some of the code in some slightly different way so they can be integrated. This should have no effect on C mode, and minimal effect on SC performance on large designs. This seems like a good trade-off to keep maintenance and model architecture cleaner. My hope would be that almost nothing except the wrapper emitter should need to know about generating for SC. Maybe there are some technical reasons this is infeasible?
